items.title: Police have shot dead fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman in the state's north-east
items.summary: Victoria Police says he was shot by officers at a property this morning.

items.title: Cameron Green unable to bowl in IPL opener, Ajinkya Rahane calls out Cricket Australia
items.summary: After becoming the most expensive overseas purchase in IPL history, Australian Cameron Green is apparently not allowed to bowl in Kolkata's opening IPL match.

items.title: Nepal's former prime minister arrested over deaths during protests
items.summary: Nepalese police arrests former prime minister Khadga Prasad Oli as they investigate whether he was negligent in failing to prevent dozens of deaths during a crackdown on anti-corruption protests last September.

items.title: Tottenham Hotspur parts ways with Igor Tudor as disastrous EPL season continues following Ange Postecoglou sacking
items.summary: Tottenham announces it has parted ways with interim manager Igor Tudor as the EPL giant fights to avoid relegation.

items.title: Festival encourages people to see seaweed's 'importance and beauty'
items.summary: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

items.title: Paralympic athletes face a 'double whammy' of disability and gender discrimination
items.summary: Sport isn't "all roses" for many disabled and chronically ill women in elite sport, as they grapple with discrimination around their health and wellbeing.

items.title: Hand-raised baby corals bring hope of restoring Great Barrier Reef
items.summary: Tens of thousands of baby corals, grown on land or settled from wild spawn slicks, have been transplanted onto the Great Barrier Reef.

items.title: Violent organised crime syndicates linked to illegal tobacco may be coming to Darwin

items.summary: Farmers, fishers and business leaders are appealing to the federal and state governments to provide targeted relief to help them deal with massive fuel price increases caused by the Iran War, and the looming prospect of shortages.

items.title: 'Plenty of blue skies' expected in Queensland over Easter weekend with some 'stop-start rainfall'
items.summary: Mostly sunny, cool Easter weather is expected for much of Queensland, with only brief coastal showers, while far-north areas such as Cairns can expect heavier, more persistent rain.

items.title: Electric trucks trade diesel for batteries to make their mark on Australian roads

items.title: Adriana Rivas, the Bondi nanny accused of torture for Pinochet, makes last stand to evade extradition to Chile

items.title: The strange death of Liberal England and the lessons for Liberal Adelaide

As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

items.title: Measles on the rise across NSW, concern community infections spreading undetected
items.summary: An outbreak of measles in south-east Asia has fuelled a surge in NSW cases — but the state's biggest cause for concern is five infections believed to have been spread undetected in the community.

items.title: Critically endangered hawksbill sea turtle returns to wild months after beach rescue
items.summary: What passers-by thought was a "sandy rock" was in fact a critically endangered turtle far from home.

items.title: Hawksbill sea turtle returns to ocean after months-long recovery

items.title: Iran's soccer team honours victims of deadly airstrike on primary school
items.summary: Iran soccer players hold small backpacks to honour the victims of a deadly missile strike on a girls' primary school, before a friendly against Nigeria.

items.title: Australian Iranians hit back over 'racist' decision to block 7,000 tourist visa holders
items.summary: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

items.title: Treaty elections underway in Victoria as changes flow from nation-leading agreement
items.summary: Victoria's treaty elections are underway, with successful candidates taking on new responsibilities. But uncertainty remains, with the opposition sticking to its pledge to scrap the agreement if elected in November.

items.title: Katherine residents call for hospital relocation after flood evacuations
items.summary: After Katherine's worst flood in 28 years, residents are calling for governments to act on a decades-old idea to relocate the NT town's hospital away from the flood-prone river.

items.title: NSW Waratahs snap losing streak with Super Rugby win over ACT Brumbies in Canberra
items.summary: NSW Waratahs coach Dan McKellar heralds his side's much-needed 30-28 victory against bitter rivals the ACT Brumbies as a special memory for the club.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis launch first missiles on Israel as Middle East war intensifies
items.summary: The Iranian-backed militant group took to X to make the announcement, saying it launched a salvo of ballistic rockets at military targets in southern Israel.

items.title: Growing pains as blueberry industry expands rapidly and appetites increase
items.summary: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.

items.title: Pakistan's conflict with Afghan Taliban intensifies in shadow of Middle East war
items.summary: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

items.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer — yet most still miss the early signs
items.summary: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

items.title: The community behind Geelong music festival Jerkfest's unlikely success
items.summary: As music festivals collapse, one Geelong kid's hobby has turned into a big day out.

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn
items.summary: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

items.title: US 'on verge' of ending Iran war as allies warned to lead postwar security coalition in the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells G7 leaders to ready a postwar coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the US-Israeli war on Iran could conclude within weeks.

items.title: Donald Trump wants a 'silver bullet' in Iran but he's dealing with a 'very complex situation'
items.summary: Donald Trump is deploying thousands of marines and extra battleships to the Middle East, while also claiming to be close to a deal with Iran to end the war. So is he set to wind down his war with Iran, or is he thinking of escalating?

items.title: Online travel site Guest Reservations accused of fleecing customers with hidden fees and charges
items.summary: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

items.title: TGA head defends handling of Australia's sunscreen scandal over SPF test results
items.summary: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

items.title: Claims gas exporters on 'best behaviour' as government intervention looms
items.summary: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

items.summary: The federal government is turning its mind to what Australian support in the Middle East might look like after a ceasefire.

items.summary: While markets struggle to price a sudden collapse in oil supply, military analysts are sounding the alarm on the rate countries are burning through weapons stockpiles.

items.title: Socceroos snatch late win against Cameroon as Jordy Bos delivers and teenager Lucas Herrington impresses

items.title: Proposed new laws allow police to move people on for 'causing anxiety' without committing a crime
items.summary: Young people could be moved on by police officers for "causing anxiety" without ever committing a crime under proposed Queensland laws.

items.title: Magpies confirm 'hamstring awareness' for Moore after win over Giants
items.summary: Collingwood is expecting to be without its skipper Darcy Moore and his predecessor Scott Pendlebury next week against Brisbane after the pair were hobbled during the win over GWS.

items.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues

items.summary: The Broncos overpowered the Dolphins 26-22 in the Battle of Brisbane, after Wests Tigers stunned ladder leaders New Zealand Warriors in Auckland. Follow live.

items.title: Kelpie with head wounds found tied to tree at Adelaide Hills golf course
items.summary: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information. Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

items.title: Queensland opposition says government broke promise on adult crime adult time advice
items.summary: A report from the expert legal panel advising the state government's adult crime, adult time policy has been released, explaining the framework for the advice, rather than everything provided to the government.

items.title: Diesel fuel stolen after thieves break into rural property in central Tasmania

items.summary: New South Wales beaches will be too dangerous to visit this weekend, with authorities issuing a warning to rock fishers, swimmers and boaters to stay away from the coastline.

items.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries

items.summary: Police say the charges come amid a concerning rise in the use of 3D printing technology to manufacture weapons.

items.summary: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle continues to cut a destructive swathe through Western Australia's Gascoyne even as it weakens, with authorities warning the extreme weather is not over yet.

items.title: New Spirit of Tasmania ferry to be Dark Mofo 2026 floating art gallery
items.summary: The new spirit of Tasmania ferries might not be available for journeys across the Bass Strait yet — but this winter, one of them will take on a new role as an art gallery, during Hobart's Dark Mofo festival.

items.title: AFL admits Adelaide wronged by crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Geelong
items.summary: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

items.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects
items.summary: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

items.title: Can states cap fuel prices? Tasmanian emergency fuel laws explained
items.summary: The Tasmanian government is seeking to have the power to cap petrol prices. But how will it work? And if it does, can other states do the same?

items.title: Former ALP politician Milton Orkopoulos plans appeal against sex abuse convictions
items.summary: Paedophile and former NSW Labor politician Milton Orkopoulos says he has Legal Aid funding to explore an appeal against his convictions for abusing seven boys.

items.title: Fuel surcharges across a broad range of industries are adding to consumers' financial stress
items.summary: Fuel surcharges are being applied across a range of industries, from the delivery of bricks to building sites to Australia's biggest rideshare companies, meaning consumer prices are rising.

items.summary: Kakadu residents whose homes have been submerged by roof-high floodwater said they feel like they've been forgotten, with evacuation centres and relief payments not made available to them.

items.title: Conflict reshapes Persian New Year for Iranian Australians
items.summary: As Iranian Australians try to celebrate Persian new year, news of the conflict in the Middle East is always front of mind.

items.title: PM leaves door open to halving fuel excise temporarily
items.summary: The prime minister leaves the door open to halving taxes on petrol and diesel, after the Coalition demands temporary relief from sky-high fuel prices.

items.title: Mould infection cluster report identifies RPA balcony as likely exposure site, one patient remains in ICU
items.summary: A balcony at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital has been identified as the infection site of a mould cluster linked to two patient deaths late last year.

items.title: Gladstone's Jake Lyle to star in Phantom of the Opera on Sydney Harbour

items.title: More than 1,000 Australians physically assaulted every day, new ABS crime data shows
items.summary: Almost 400,000 Australians experienced physical assault in 2024-2025, according to new victimisation data released by the Australian Bureau of Statistics.

items.summary: The national peak body for aged care providers is calling on the federal government to provide urgent support to keep services running, as the Middle East war sends fuel prices skyrocketing.

items.title: Enriched uranium hidden deep in Iranian mountains could be key to ending war

items.title: 'Surreal' feeling as snow falls in March across parts of New South Wales
items.summary: It is still only March, but snow is falling in parts of NSW and the Bureau of Meteorology is checking its record books.

items.title: 'QuitGPT' boycott could spark AI flashpoint as ethical use debate rages
items.summary: AI companies are stuck in a "perfect storm" of trying to control the use and output of their platforms while maintaining competitive profits, as millions of people boycott the technology, experts say.

items.title: How Australians interact with birds and wildlife around their homes
items.summary: Our recent stories about attracting native fauna to gardens and feeding wild birds resonated strongly. Here is how some Australians interact with wildlife in their local areas.

items.title: Big Red Bash cancelled for 2026, outback tourism facing uncertain season as fuel prices bite
items.summary: This festival usually sees people go all out, spending thousands of dollars and travelling thousands of kilometres, but a potential change in location and fuel concerns have deterred ticket holders.

items.title: Cape York stations hit by Cyclone Narelle call for army's help to clean up

items.title: Goulburn community solar farm plugs in after 12-year journey
items.summary: Hundreds of residents of a rural NSW town combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

items.title: Boat master jailed over illegally caught sea cucumbers in Far North Queensland
items.summary: Authorities found almost two tonnes of the lucrative sea cucumbers on board a foreign fishing boat that was hidden in mangroves near a remote river in the country's far north earlier this month.

items.title: Wind power off Victoria's coast could reduce need for new transmission lines, report finds
items.summary: Developing Victoria's wind power sector could save the state money and powerline infrastructure, while providing stability for Eastern Australia's power grid, a new report shows.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle smashes west Pilbara coast, tearing off roofs in Exmouth
items.summary: Residents on WA's north-west coast are feeling the full brunt of severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it barrels across the region, tearing off roofs and flooding streets, with the system now downgraded to a category three.

items.title: Petition calls for release of police video of Indigenous man's death in custody
items.summary: Raylene Nixon says the public should be able to make their own minds up about what happened to her son in his final moments.
